# Spec: v2 graph engine — refactor cc-master:kanban-add to query graph for dedup

## Requirement

Refactor `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` so the pre-create dedup step is driven by a Cypher query against the Kuzu graph at `.cc-master/graph.kuzu/`, with a strict JSON fallback per `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md` whenever any pre-query check fails. `kanban-add` is the SECOND graph integration target and the FIRST graph-backed WRITE-PATH integration — it both reads the graph (for dedup) and writes to `kanban.json`, so it also establishes the post-write invalidation pattern that Wave 8 (#18) will generalize across all 22 write-side skills. After every `kanban.json` write, the skill MUST invoke `cc-master:index --touch .cc-master/kanban.json` to refresh the derived index. This is the proof-of-pattern for the write-invalidate contract.

Two tracks ship in the same task:

1. **Dedup read path** — replace the current implicit dedup (documented at `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md:23` and the "do not create duplicate tasks" rule at line 339) with an explicit graph query: `MATCH (t:Task) WHERE toLower(t.subject) CONTAINS toLower($subject_fragment) AND t.source = $source RETURN t.id AS id, t.subject AS subject`. When the graph is absent, stale, or errors, fall back to reading `kanban.json` and performing the same subject-overlap check in memory. In both modes, the skill surfaces any candidate overlaps to the user via AskUserQuestion BEFORE writing new tasks — overlap is not silent.

2. **Write-invalidate pattern** — after every branch that ends with a `kanban.json` write (Mode 1 roadmap import, Mode 2 insights import, Mode 3 manual add, and the `--add-gh-issues` link-back update), shell out to `/cc-master:index --touch .cc-master/kanban.json`. On non-zero exit, surface a one-line warning to the user and continue — task creation MUST succeed even if graph invalidation fails, because the JSON is the source of truth and the next graph query will fall back per protocol. This is the explicit proof-of-pattern referenced in the Wave 4 brief.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` gains a new section `## Graph-Backed Dedup` placed immediately after the existing `## Input Validation Rules` and before `## GitHub Issue Creation`. The section cites `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md` verbatim per that file's Citation Pattern block and runs the three pre-query checks.
2. The dedup section defines a helper procedure `dedup_candidates(subject_fragment, source)` that: (a) attempts the Cypher query `MATCH (t:Task) WHERE toLower(t.subject) CONTAINS toLower($subject_fragment) AND t.source = $source RETURN t.id AS id, t.subject AS subject` via `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py query` using `--params-json`, (b) on any pre-query check failure OR exit-code-4 from `kuzu_client.py`, emits one session-scoped fallback warning (`"Graph absent/stale — falling back to JSON read for kanban.json"`) and performs the same filter against the JSON task list in memory, (c) returns an array of `{id, subject}` objects. The procedure is idempotent and side-effect-free.
3. The helper is invoked from each of the three modes before any `kanban.json` write:
   - **Mode 1 (from roadmap):** for each selected feature, call `dedup_candidates(feature.title, "roadmap")`. If candidates exist, surface them via AskUserQuestion with options "Skip this one", "Add anyway", "Stop". "Add anyway" proceeds with creation; "Skip" drops that feature from the batch; "Stop" exits without any writes.
   - **Mode 2 (from insights):** same pattern as Mode 1 but with `source = "insights"`.
   - **Mode 3 (manual):** call `dedup_candidates(title, "manual")` before create. If candidates exist, same three-way prompt.
4. A new `## Post-Write Invalidation` section is added immediately after `## GitHub Issue Creation`. It describes the `--touch` invocation contract: after ANY `kanban.json` write (initial creation, blocked_by updates, `gh_issue_number` link-back), run `/cc-master:index --touch .cc-master/kanban.json` via the Skill tool. On exit code 0, silent success. On non-zero exit, emit one warning line of the form `"Warning: graph invalidation failed (exit code <N>) — next graph-backed skill will fall back to JSON. Run /cc-master:index --full to rebuild."` and continue. Task creation outcomes are UNAFFECTED by invalidation failure.
5. Every one of the three mode sections (Mode 1, Mode 2, Mode 3) explicitly references the `## Post-Write Invalidation` section at the point in its step sequence where `kanban.json` write completes. The references are surgical — a one-line instruction `"Run the Post-Write Invalidation step."` inserted at the correct numbered step, not a copy-paste of the whole section.
6. The `--add-gh-issues` flow's link-back write (which updates `metadata.gh_issue_number` and `metadata.gh_issue_url` on an already-created task) also triggers the Post-Write Invalidation step. This covers the case where the graph was fresh after initial creation but the subsequent metadata mutation re-invalidated the hash.
7. Batch efficiency: if Mode 1 creates N roadmap tasks in a single pass, the skill invokes `--touch` exactly ONCE at the end of the batch, not N times. Same for Mode 2 and for the `--add-gh-issues` link-back loop — coalesce into one invalidation call per kanban.json write session. Mode 3 (manual, single task) invokes `--touch` exactly once per task.
8. No read-side behavior regressions: the existing `metadata.feature_id` check for roadmap imports (currently in Step 5 of Mode 1) stays in place as a distinct safeguard — the graph-backed subject-overlap check is ADDITIVE, not a replacement. `feature_id` catches re-imports of the same roadmap feature; subject overlap catches humans typing near-duplicates across modes.
9. Input validation: `subject_fragment` passed to the Cypher query MUST be the LITERAL subject string, bound via `--params-json` (never string-concatenated into the Cypher text). The `kuzu_client.py query` call MUST use `--params-json '{"subject_fragment": "...", "source": "..."}'` — Cypher injection is not a theoretical risk here because subjects come from the user, but the parameter-binding contract is the only safe pattern and MUST be established for all future write-side integrations.
10. No read-side writes to the graph: the dedup step does NOT issue DDL, MERGE, CREATE, or any side-effecting Cypher. The ONLY write path to the graph is via `/cc-master:index --touch` invoked AFTER a completed `kanban.json` write. Verification: `grep -E 'kuzu_client\.py (init|close|.*MERGE|.*CREATE |.*DELETE|.*MATCH.*SET)' skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` MUST return zero lines.

## Production Readiness (mandatory — every spec)

1. **No stub data:** The dedup query MUST run against the real `.cc-master/graph.kuzu/` directory produced by `/cc-master:index` and reflect real task data from `.cc-master/kanban.json`. The skill MUST NOT hardcode a sample "similar tasks: [...]" fallback, MUST NOT return an empty array without actually running either the Cypher query or the JSON scan, and MUST NOT ship a version that says "dedup check stubbed — TODO integrate graph". The `--touch` invocation MUST call the real `/cc-master:index` Skill — not a mock, not a print statement pretending to invalidate.
2. **No skeleton functions:** The `dedup_candidates` helper MUST do real work — it must actually shell out to `kuzu_client.py query` (or open and iterate kanban.json on the fallback path), actually filter by `toLower(subject) CONTAINS toLower(fragment) AND source = <source>`, and actually return the matched rows. The three-way "skip / add anyway / stop" AskUserQuestion MUST be presented and honored — skipping MUST drop the feature from the batch, stopping MUST exit before any writes, and "add anyway" MUST proceed with the existing create flow unchanged. The Post-Write Invalidation helper MUST actually call `/cc-master:index --touch`, inspect its exit code, and emit the warning on non-zero.
3. **No TODO/FIXME/HACK:** Zero TODO, FIXME, HACK, STUB, PLACEHOLDER, or SKELETON markers in `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` after this refactor.
4. **Real integrations:** The skill shells into the real `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py` via Bash for the dedup query. The fallback reads the real `.cc-master/kanban.json` via the Read tool. The post-write invalidation invokes the real `cc-master:index` Skill via the Skill tool with `skill: "cc-master:index"` and `args: "--touch .cc-master/kanban.json"`. No mocks, no pretend-calls.
5. **Real error handling:** Every `kuzu_client.py` invocation checks exit code per the protocol's Failure Modes table (exit 2 → install hint + JSON fallback; exit 3 → graph-missing hint + JSON fallback; exit 4 → one-line diagnostic + JSON fallback). The `/cc-master:index --touch` invocation checks exit code and emits the "graph invalidation failed" warning on any non-zero value. AskUserQuestion cancellation is handled as "Stop" (exit without writes). JSON fallback failure (kanban.json malformed during the fallback scan) surfaces a clear error and exits non-zero — no silent wrong-answer paths.
6. **Client test:** If a paying cc-master user has 200 tasks in their kanban and runs `/cc-master:kanban-add "Add user authentication"`, the skill MUST detect any existing task with "authentication" in the subject (graph mode, sub-second), prompt via AskUserQuestion, and (on "Add anyway") create the task AND invalidate the graph so the next `/cc-master:kanban` render reflects reality. If the user then deletes `.cc-master/graph.kuzu/` and re-runs the same command, the fallback path MUST detect the overlap via JSON scan, prompt identically, and create the task. If either path ships a stale board or a duplicate task, the implementation is not done.

## Technical Approach

### Pattern Reference

Follow the pattern established in `skills/kanban/SKILL.md` (Wave 3 — the first graph-backed read-side integration) for:
- Citation-block placement and wording (see `skills/kanban/SKILL.md` Step 1b — copy the block verbatim).
- Pre-query check ordering and exit-code branching.
- The one-warning-per-session rule.

Follow the pattern established in `skills/index/SKILL.md` (the `## --touch Single-File Refresh` section, starting around line 1009) for:
- How `--touch` is invoked and what its exit codes mean.
- The single-summary-line output contract that makes `--touch` safely callable from other skills.

### Files to Modify

- `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` — three additions:
  1. New section `## Graph-Backed Dedup` between `## Input Validation Rules` and `## GitHub Issue Creation`. Contains the citation block, the three pre-query checks, the `dedup_candidates(subject_fragment, source)` helper contract (describing Cypher with `--params-json` and the JSON fallback branch), and the AskUserQuestion wording for the three-way skip/add/stop prompt.
  2. New section `## Post-Write Invalidation` between `## GitHub Issue Creation` and `## Mode 1: From Roadmap`. Describes the `/cc-master:index --touch .cc-master/kanban.json` invocation, the exit-code inspection, and the warning wording on non-zero exit.
  3. Surgical edits inside Mode 1, Mode 2, Mode 3 step blocks: insert a `"Before creation, call dedup_candidates(...)"` step and a `"Run the Post-Write Invalidation step"` line at the points where kanban.json is written. Do NOT duplicate the helper content in-line; reference the `## Graph-Backed Dedup` and `## Post-Write Invalidation` sections by name.

- Also touch Mode 1 Step 6 (the blocked_by wiring), Mode 2 Step 5 (the remove-from-pending-suggestions write), and the `--add-gh-issues` metadata link-back loop so that invalidation runs exactly once per kanban.json mutation session — NOT once per task inside a loop. Explicit: batch writes coalesce to one `--touch`.

### Files to Create

None. This refactor is pure skill-markdown edits. No new fixtures, no new scripts — all infrastructure (`kuzu_client.py`, `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md`, `--touch` flag on `cc-master:index`) already exists from Waves 1–3.

### Verified API Contracts

No HTTP/service boundary is crossed. All operations are local:

- **Cypher query**: `python3 scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py query <db_path> <cypher> --params-json <json>` — stdout is a JSON array of row objects. Exit 0 on success. Exit 4 on Cypher error. Exit 2 if kuzu binding missing. Exit 3 if graph path absent. Source: `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py:94-141` (cmd_query).
- **Touch invocation**: `Skill tool with skill: "cc-master:index", args: "--touch .cc-master/kanban.json"`. Exit 0 = changed/unchanged/deleted. Exit 2 = arg validation failure (should not happen — path is a literal constant). Exit 3 = Kuzu DB issue. Exit 4 = Cypher error during touch. Source: `skills/index/SKILL.md` lines 1125-1137 (the touch exit-code contract table).

### Dependencies

None beyond what Waves 1–3 already shipped. `pip install kuzu==0.11.2` (Wave 1), `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md` (Wave 3, Task 7), `cc-master:index --touch` flag (Wave 2).

### Database Changes

None. The Task node schema in the indexer already carries `id`, `subject`, `source`, and the other fields the dedup query needs (confirmed against the Wave 3 Task DDL in `skills/index/SKILL.md`). No ALTER, no migration, no rebuild required.

## Verification

- [ ] `grep -c 'prompts/graph-read-protocol.md' skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` returns at least 1.
- [ ] `grep -cE '(TODO|FIXME|HACK|STUB|PLACEHOLDER|SKELETON)' skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` returns 0.
- [ ] `grep -cE 'kuzu_client\.py (init|close|.*MERGE|.*CREATE |.*DELETE|.*MATCH.*SET)' skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` returns 0 (no write-side Cypher leaks into the skill).
- [ ] `grep -c '/cc-master:index --touch' skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` returns at least 1.
- [ ] `grep -c '## Graph-Backed Dedup' skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` returns exactly 1.
- [ ] `grep -c '## Post-Write Invalidation' skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md` returns exactly 1.
- [ ] Manual test A (graph present, overlap exists): with the v2-graph-engine build graph-indexed, run `/cc-master:kanban-add` in manual mode with a subject that overlaps an existing task → AskUserQuestion three-way prompt fires → "Stop" exits without write.
- [ ] Manual test B (graph present, no overlap): same setup, novel subject → no prompt, task created, `/cc-master:index --touch` runs, next `/cc-master:kanban` shows the new task with `Rendered from: graph` (confirming the invalidation refreshed the hash).
- [ ] Manual test C (graph absent): `mv .cc-master/graph.kuzu/ /tmp/graph-backup-$$/`, then `/cc-master:kanban-add` with an overlapping subject → exactly one "Graph absent/stale — falling back to JSON read for kanban.json" warning, dedup still catches the overlap, three-way prompt fires. Restore with `mv /tmp/graph-backup-$$/ .cc-master/graph.kuzu/`.
- [ ] Manual test D (touch fails gracefully): temporarily break the graph directory mid-session (e.g., `chmod 000 .cc-master/graph.kuzu/`), run `/cc-master:kanban-add` manual mode → task IS created in kanban.json, warning line about graph invalidation failure is emitted, exit is non-zero or zero per the contract (zero — task creation succeeded). Restore permissions.

## Risks

- **Risk (MEDIUM):** The three-way AskUserQuestion prompt adds friction to every `kanban-add` invocation that happens to overlap an existing subject. **Mitigation:** The query is narrowly scoped (`CONTAINS` on toLower subject fragment + exact source match), so overlaps should be rare except when they matter. If UX testing shows the prompt fires too often for near-duplicates that are actually distinct, tighten the fragment (e.g., require 10+ character overlap, or use token-based matching) in a follow-up task. Do NOT expand scope in this task.
- **Risk (MEDIUM):** If `/cc-master:index --touch` is slow (>500ms on a large project), manual `kanban-add` becomes perceptibly laggy. **Mitigation:** The touch-path target in `skills/index/SKILL.md:1025` is <500ms changed-path, which is well within UX tolerance. If the measurement in Wave 3 showed touch latency over budget, that is an infrastructure issue to fix in Wave 8's systematization pass — not here.
- **Risk (LOW):** The Cypher parameter-binding path (`--params-json`) is new for cc-master skills — prior Wave 3 queries were parameter-less. **Mitigation:** `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py:101-112` already supports `--params-json`. The dedup query is the first real consumer and the pattern is standard. Verify during build by running a manual Cypher with `--params-json` against the live graph.
- **Risk (LOW):** A future skill that mutates kanban.json outside `kanban-add` (e.g., `build` marking a task `in_progress`, `qa-review` recording iteration) forgets to invalidate. **Mitigation:** Wave 8 (#18) systematizes this across all 22 write-side skills. This spec just establishes the pattern here as the first concrete example. Do NOT preemptively refactor other skills in this task — stay scope-locked.
- **Risk (LOW):** The `--add-gh-issues` link-back writes `metadata.gh_issue_number`/`metadata.gh_issue_url` per task in a loop. If invalidation fires per-task instead of once at the end, N GitHub Issues = N touch calls. **Mitigation:** Acceptance Criterion 7 explicitly requires coalescing to a single `--touch` per batch. The spec calls this out, and the build subtask below must implement it correctly.

## Subtasks

1. Add `## Graph-Backed Dedup` section with citation block and helper contract — insert the new section between `## Input Validation Rules` and `## GitHub Issue Creation`. Paste the citation block from `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md` verbatim. Define the `dedup_candidates(subject_fragment, source)` helper: pre-query checks in order, Cypher query with `--params-json`, fallback to JSON-scan of `kanban.json` filtering on `toLower(t.subject).contains(toLower(subject_fragment))` and `t.metadata.source == source`, return `[{id, subject}, ...]`. Define the three-way AskUserQuestion wording ("Skip this one" / "Add anyway" / "Stop") and its semantics. No Cypher injection — use `--params-json` only. Session-scoped warning on fallback (at most one per session).
   Files: `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md`
   Depends on: none

   Production Readiness scope for this subtask: helper MUST do real work (real Cypher call with `--params-json`, real JSON scan on fallback, real return of matched rows). No stub data. No TODO markers.

2. Add `## Post-Write Invalidation` section with exit-code contract — insert the new section between `## GitHub Issue Creation` and `## Mode 1: From Roadmap`. Specify the invocation: Skill tool with `skill: "cc-master:index"`, `args: "--touch .cc-master/kanban.json"`. Document exit-code handling per the touch-path contract in `skills/index/SKILL.md` lines 1125-1137. Specify the warning wording on non-zero exit: `"Warning: graph invalidation failed (exit code <N>) — next graph-backed skill will fall back to JSON. Run /cc-master:index --full to rebuild."`. Invalidation failure MUST NOT block task creation — the JSON write already succeeded and is the source of truth.
   Files: `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md`
   Depends on: none

   Production Readiness scope: real Skill tool invocation, real exit-code check, real warning. No pretend-touch, no "TODO wire up invalidation".

3. Wire dedup + invalidation into Mode 1 (From Roadmap) — edit Mode 1's step block. After Step 4 (resolve competitor evidence) and before Step 5 (create tasks), insert a `"Call dedup_candidates(feature.title, \"roadmap\") for each selected feature. On overlap, use AskUserQuestion. Drop skipped features, stop on Stop."` step. After Step 7 (update roadmap.json) and after Step 8 (the --add-gh-issues loop, if present), add a single `"Run the Post-Write Invalidation step."` call. Explicit: invalidation fires ONCE at the end of the batch, not per-feature. The `--add-gh-issues` link-back loop also triggers one consolidated invalidation at its end.
   Files: `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md`
   Depends on: 1, 2

   Production Readiness scope: real batch coalescing (one `--touch` per mode pass), real three-way prompt handling, no duplicate task creation on overlap-then-skip.

4. Wire dedup + invalidation into Mode 2 (From Insights) and Mode 3 (Manual) — mirror the Mode 1 wiring. Mode 2: dedup check before task creation, single invalidation after the pending-suggestions.json update and the kanban.json write. Mode 3: dedup check after the user provides title and priority but before the write, single invalidation after the write (including after the `--add-gh-issues` link-back if present). Mode 3 is a single task so "batch" and "single" collapse — one `--touch`.
   Files: `skills/kanban-add/SKILL.md`
   Depends on: 1, 2

   Production Readiness scope: real prompt handling in both modes, real invalidation, no silent drops.

5. Manual verification on this repository — exercise all four test scenarios from `## Verification` (graph-present-with-overlap, graph-present-no-overlap, graph-absent-with-overlap, touch-fails). Record the outcomes in the build summary. If any test fails, loop into qa-fix before declaring the task complete. Append a bullet to the project MEMORY.md under the Graph Engine section: `"Wave 4 (#9) shipped graph-backed dedup + post-write invalidation in kanban-add. Proof-of-pattern for the write-invalidate contract systematized in Wave 8."`
   Files: project `MEMORY.md` (append-only) — no source code changes in this subtask
   Depends on: 3, 4

   Production Readiness scope: real end-to-end tests against the live graph and the live JSON fallback, not a dry-run or grep-only verification.
